q: Those who cannot change their minds cannot change anything.
h:
“Those who cannot change their minds cannot change anything.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: How much pain they have cost us, the evils which have never happened.
h:
“How much pain they have cost us, the evils which have never happened.” —
Thomas Jefferson

q: I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand.
h:
“I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand. ” —
Confucius

q: Art, like morality, consists of drawing the line somewhere.
h:
“Art, like morality, consists of drawing the line somewhere.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: Curiosity is the most powerful thing you own. Imagination is a force that can actually manifest a reality.
h:
“Curiosity is the most powerful thing you own. Imagination is a force that can actually manifest a reality.” —
James Cameron

q: The soul without imagination is what an observatory would be without a telescope.
h:
“The soul without imagination is what an observatory would be without a telescope.” —
Henry Ward Beecher

q: Even in the grave, all is not lost.
h:
“Even in the grave, all is not lost.” —
Edgar Allan Poe

q: Any fool can paint a picture, but it takes a wise man to be able to sell it.
h:
“Any fool can paint a picture, but it takes a wise man to be able to sell it.” —
Samuel Butler

q: Every second you have on this planet is very precious and it's your responsibility that you're happy.
h:
“Every second you have on this planet is very precious and it's your responsibility that you're happy.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
h:
“Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.” —
Aristotle

q: Better to die fighting for freedom than be a prisoner all the days of your life.
h:
“Better to die fighting for freedom than be a prisoner all the days of your life.” —
Bob Marley

q: Focus on how far you have come in life rather than looking at the accomplishments of others.
h:
“Focus on how far you have come in life rather than looking at the accomplishments of others.” —
Lolly Daskal

q: The most important thing in communication is to hear what isn't being said.
h:
“The most important thing in communication is to hear what isn't being said.” —
Peter Drucker

q: If we will be quiet and ready enough, we shall find compensation in every disappointment.
h:
“If we will be quiet and ready enough, we shall find compensation in every disappointment.” —
Henry David Thoreau

q: What you do today can improve all your tomorrows.
h:
“What you do today can improve all your tomorrows.” —
Ralph Marston

q: Success is doing what you want to do, when you want, where you want, with whom you want, as much as you want.
h:
“Success is doing what you want to do, when you want, where you want, with whom you want, as much as you want.” —
Tony Robbins

q: A day without laughter is a day wasted.
h:
“A day without laughter is a day wasted.” —
Charlie Chaplin

q: A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.
h:
“A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: A gentleman is one who puts more into the world than he takes out.
h:
“A gentleman is one who puts more into the world than he takes out.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: I have lived with several Zen masters -- all of them cats.
h:
“I have lived with several Zen masters -- all of them cats.” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: Without struggle, no progress and no result. Every breaking of habit produces a change in the machine.
h:
“Without struggle, no progress and no result. Every breaking of habit produces a change in the machine.” —
G.I. Gurdjieff

q: It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong.
h:
“It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong.” —
Voltaire

q: There's no next time. It's now or never.
h:
“There's no next time. It's now or never.” —
Celestine Chua

q: That some achieve great success is proof to all that others can achieve it as well.
h:
“That some achieve great success is proof to all that others can achieve it as well.” —
Abraham Lincoln

q: No one can compete with you on being you. Most of life is a search for who and what needs you the most.
h:
“No one can compete with you on being you. Most of life is a search for who and what needs you the most.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: When you want to be honored by others, you learn to honor them first.
h:
“When you want to be honored by others, you learn to honor them first.” —
Sathya Sai Baba

q: If you try to get rid of fear and anger without knowing their meaning, they will grow stronger and return.
h:
“If you try to get rid of fear and anger without knowing their meaning, they will grow stronger and return.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: In the middle of chaos lies opportunity.
h:
“In the middle of chaos lies opportunity. ” —
Bruce Lee

q: Correct what you can. Learn from what you can't.
h:
“Correct what you can. Learn from what you can't.” —
Toni Morrison

q: If you believe you can, you can. If you believe you can't, then, well you can't.
h:
“If you believe you can, you can. If you believe you can't, then, well you can't.” —
Celestine Chua

q: I am who I am today because of the choices I made yesterday.
h:
“I am who I am today because of the choices I made yesterday.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: Find your Light; They can't love you if they can't see you.
h:
“Find your Light; They can't love you if they can't see you.” —
Bette Midler

q: Fear is stupid. So are regrets.
h:
“Fear is stupid. So are regrets.” —
Marilyn Monroe

q: Rest in reason; move in passion.
h:
“Rest in reason; move in passion.” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: Unless someone truly has the power to say no, they never truly have the power to say yes.
h:
“Unless someone truly has the power to say no, they never truly have the power to say yes.” —
Dan Millman

q: Knowledge is a treasure, but practice is the key to it.
h:
“Knowledge is a treasure, but practice is the key to it.” —
Lao Tzu

q: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.
h:
“When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.” —
Lao Tzu

q: Winning isn't always championships.
h:
“Winning isn't always championships.” —
Michael Jordan

q: There is no absolute success in the world, only constant progress.
h:
“There is no absolute success in the world, only constant progress.” —
Jonathan Swift

q: All happiness depends on courage and work.
h:
“All happiness depends on courage and work.” —
Honore de Balzac

q: You are the only problem you will ever have and you are the only solution.
h:
“You are the only problem you will ever have and you are the only solution.” —
Bob Proctor

q: The outer conditions of a person's life will always be found to reflect their inner beliefs.
h:
“The outer conditions of a person's life will always be found to reflect their inner beliefs.” —
James Allen

q: You win more from losing than winning.
h:
“You win more from losing than winning.” —
Morgan Wootten

q: The greatest weariness comes from work not done.
h:
“The greatest weariness comes from work not done.” —
Eric Hoffer

q: If you put water into a cup, it becomes the cup.
h:
“If you put water into a cup, it becomes the cup.” —
Bruce Lee

q: Never to suffer would never to have been blessed.
h:
“Never to suffer would never to have been blessed.” —
Edgar Allan Poe

q: The great use of life is to spend it for something that will outlast it.
h:
“The great use of life is to spend it for something that will outlast it.” —
William James

q: The secret of happiness, you see, is not found in seeking more, but in developing the capacity to enjoy less.
h:
“The secret of happiness, you see, is not found in seeking more, but in developing the capacity to enjoy less.” —
Dan Millman

q: If you truly love Nature, you will find beauty everywhere.
h:
“If you truly love Nature, you will find beauty everywhere. ” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Everyone sees what you appear to be, few experience what you really are.
h:
“Everyone sees what you appear to be, few experience what you really are.” —
Niccolo Machiavelli
